items.headline: U.S. Stocks Hover Near Record Highs Amid Geopolitical Optimism and Strong Earnings
items.summary: U.S. equity markets saw mixed movements today following a strong Wednesday session where the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite reached new record highs. Optimism surrounding a potential U.S.-Iran deal and robust first-quarter earnings from major companies are buoying sentiment, despite warnings about lingering geopolitical risks and potential inflation. Technology and communication services led gains, while healthcare and financials lagged. Not financial advice.

items.headline: S&P 500
items.statContext: Up 0.23% in intraday trading. Closed at a record 7,022.95 on Wednesday, up 0.8%.

items.headline: NASDAQ Composite
items.statContext: Gained 47 points today. Finished above 24,000 on Wednesday, rising 1.6% after an 11th consecutive gain.

items.headline: Dow Jones Industrial Average
items.statContext: Gained 86 points today. Slipped 0.2% to close at 48,463.72 on Wednesday.

items.headline: Aehr Test Systems
items.summary: Shares surged after securing a record $41M follow-on order for burn-in testing of custom AI ASICs, reinforcing strong demand tied to data center workloads.

items.headline: ON Semiconductor
items.summary: Led gains among S&P 500 components today.

items.headline: Hims & Hers Health, Inc.
items.summary: Extended its rally as the FDA is reportedly considering moving several peptides out of restrictive categories, a potential regulatory tailwind.

items.headline: Abbott Laboratories
items.summary: Among the worst-performing stocks in the S&P 500 today.

items.headline: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
items.summary: Despite reporting a 58% jump in profit, shares saw a decline, possibly due to profit-taking or other market factors.

items.headline: Sector Performance: Tech and Communication Services Lead, Energy and Healthcare Lag
items.summary: On Wednesday, Consumer Discretionary, Information Technology, Communication Services, and Real Estate sectors showed strong performance. Today, Energy, Communication Services, and Technology stocks are leading advances among S&P 500 components. Conversely, Health Care, Financials, and Cyclicals are among the decliners. The Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) tumbled 2% on Wednesday.

items.headline: Fed Issues Enforcement Action; Economic Outlook Remains Uncertain
items.summary: The Federal Reserve Board today announced an enforcement action. Earlier this month, on April 8, the minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting from March 17–18, 2026, were released. At that meeting, the FOMC decided to maintain the target range for the federal funds rate at 3-1/2 to 3-3/4 percent. The economic outlook remains highly uncertain, particularly due to the effects of the Middle East conflict, with real GDP growth expected between 2% and 2.5% this year.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports Drive Market Activity
items.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company ($TSM) reported a significant 58% jump in first-quarter profit, beating estimates and setting new records, largely driven by strong AI demand. PepsiCo ($PEP) also exceeded expectations with its first-quarter earnings, reporting an 8.5% increase in net revenue. Other major companies scheduled to report earnings today include Abbott Laboratories ($ABT), Netflix ($NFLX), Alcoa ($AA), and Charles Schwab ($SCHW).

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions and Oil Prices Influence Market Sentiment
items.summary: Optimism around a potential U.S.-Iran deal to extend a ceasefire has significantly lifted market sentiment, contributing to recent record highs in major indices. However, oil prices have continued to climb, with Brent crude rising to $96.32 per barrel and WTI crude to $92.51 per barrel. JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon warned about the possibility of inflation reigniting due to energy shocks and increasing geopolitical risks, including tensions in the Middle East, Ukraine, and with China.
